channel.title: 2026-06-15 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-06-15 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Beyond qubits: Multilevel quantum sensing for dark matter
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. A
👤 Xiaolin Ma, Volodymyr Takhistov, Norikazu Mizuochi, and Ernst David Herbschleb
Author(s): Xiaolin Ma, Volodymyr Takhistov, Norikazu Mizuochi, and Ernst David Herbschleb Searching for ultralight dark matter requires detecting extremely weak oscillating fields, where quantum sensing is especially promising. The authors show that spin-1 nitrogen vacancy centers in diamond can harness multilevel quantum states to combine enhanced signal response with common mode noise suppression, improving sensitivity to axion-electron dark matter interactions. [Phys. Rev. A 113, L060802] Published Mon Jun 15, 2026

item.title: Beyond trotterization: Variational product formulas for quantum simulation
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Ibsal Assi, Michael Vogl, Meenu Kumari, and J. P. F. LeBlanc
Author(s): Ibsal Assi, Michael Vogl, Meenu Kumari, and J. P. F. LeBlanc The authors introduce here a variational splitting scheme for the time-evolution operator that surpasses standard Trotter-Suzuki decompositions. Unlike typical variational methods, this framework works directly in operator space, providing globally valid results irrespective of the targeted initial state. The method generalizes naturally to a wide range of applications in condensed matter physics and beyond. In particular, it enables quantum circuits with lower gate counts and higher accuracy than Trotter-based formulas, making it useful for near-term quantum computing. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 214314] Published Mon Jun 15, 2026

item.title: Parent Hamiltonians for Stabilizer Quantum Many-Body Scars
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Shane Dooley
Author(s): Shane Dooley Quantum many-body scars (QMBS) have attracted considerable interest due to their role in weak ergodicity breaking in many-body systems. We present a general construction that embeds stabilizer states as QMBS of local Hamiltonians. The method relies on a notion of factorizability of Pauli strings on … [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 240402] Published Mon Jun 15, 2026

item.title: Quantum Walks on Simplicial Complexes and Harmonic Homology: Application to Topological Data Analysis with Superpolynomial Speedups
item.description:
📚 Quantum
👤 Ryu Hayakawa, Kuo-Chin Chen, and Min-Hsiu Hsieh
Quantum 10, 2138 (2026). https://doi.org/10.22331/q-2026-06-15-2138 This work investigates whether quantum walks on simplicial complexes exhibit quantum advantages. We introduce a novel quantum walk that encodes the combinatorial Laplacian, a key object reflecting the topology of the simplicial complex. We construct a unitary encoding projecting onto the kernel of the Laplacian, representing the harmonic cycles in the complex's homology. Our efficient construction of quantum walk unitaries for clique complexes paves the way for exploring higher-order interactions within topological structures. Our construction requires O(n³łog(1/ϵ)/łambdaₖ₎ gates, where n is the number of vertices, łambdaₖ is the smallest non-zero eigenvalue of the Laplacian, and ϵ is the projection error. Our results ind…

item.title: Temporal Coarse Graining for Classical Stochastic Noise in Quantum Systems
item.description:
📚 Quantum
👤 Tameem Albash, Steve Young, and N. Tobias Jacobson
Quantum 10, 2137 (2026). https://doi.org/10.22331/q-2026-06-15-2137 Simulations of quantum systems with Hamiltonian classical stochastic noise can be challenging when the noise exhibits temporal correlations over a multitude of time scales, such as for 1/f noise in solid-state quantum information processors. Here we present an approach for simulating Hamiltonian classical stochastic noise that performs temporal coarse-graining by effectively integrating out the high-frequency components of the noise. We focus on the case where the stochastic noise can be expressed as a sum of Ornstein-Uhlenbeck processes. Temporal coarse-graining is then achieved by conditioning the stochastic process on a coarse realization of the noise, expressing the conditioned stochastic process in terms of a sum of…

item.title: Indefinite causal order in cavity quantum electrodynamics
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 Luis Octavio Castaños-Cervantes, Lorenzo M. Procopio, and Tim J. Bartley
Author(s): Luis Octavio Castaños-Cervantes, Lorenzo M. Procopio, and Tim J. Bartley Indefinite causal order (ICO) has the potential to be a new resource for quantum information processing. In most of its experiments, ICO has been investigated in a photonic platform. Here, we investigate ICO in a cavity quantum electrodynamics system composed of two cavities. Our results show that I… [Phys. Rev. Research 8, 023295] Published Mon Jun 15, 2026

item.title: Continuum Landau surface states in a non-Hermitian Weyl semimetal
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Shuxin Lin, Rimi Banerjee, Zheyu Cheng, Kohei Kawabata, Baile Zhang, and Y. D. Chong
Author(s): Shuxin Lin, Rimi Banerjee, Zheyu Cheng, Kohei Kawabata, Baile Zhang, and Y. D. Chong The surface states of certain topological phases can be linked to a quantum anomaly: the violation of a classical symmetry by a field theory via a nonconserved current. This has been generalized to the case of a non-Hermitian (NH) chiral anomaly affecting the surfaces states of an NH Weyl phase. Her… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L241404] Published Mon Jun 15, 2026

item.title: Phase diagram of amorphous quantum spin Hall insulators
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Ranadeep Roy and Yuan-Ming Lu
Author(s): Ranadeep Roy and Yuan-Ming Lu In light of recent progress in the study of amorphous topological phases, we investigate the effects of structural disorder on the topological properties of a two-dimensional quantum spin Hall insulator modeled by the Bernevig-Hughes-Zhang Hamiltonian. Using a real-space formulation of the {mathbb… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 224206] Published Mon Jun 15, 2026

item.title: Exciton-induced frequency shift in high-harmonic generation from solids
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Xiang Li, Tong Wu, Jianshi Lu, Zitan Zuo, Shengzhe Pan, Shicheng Jiang, and Jian Wu
Author(s): Xiang Li, Tong Wu, Jianshi Lu, Zitan Zuo, Shengzhe Pan, Shicheng Jiang, and Jian Wu Spectroscopy serves as a key experimental pathway for probing many-body dynamics. Among them, high-harmonic generation (HHG) spectroscopy is particularly valued for its subcycle temporal resolution. Recent investigations have confirmed that excitonic effects in crystals can significantly enhance har… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 214315] Published Mon Jun 15, 2026

item.title: Nonlinear Hall Quantum Oscillations to Probe Topological Brown-Zak Fermions in Graphene Moiré Systems
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Lett.
👤 Jinrui Zhong, Huimin Peng, Yuqing Hu, Qi Feng, Qiuli Li, Shihao Zhang, Qinsheng Wang, Jinhai Mao, Junxi Duan, and Yugui Yao
Author(s): Jinrui Zhong, Huimin Peng, Yuqing Hu, Qi Feng, Qiuli Li, Shihao Zhang, Qinsheng Wang, Jinhai Mao, Junxi Duan, and Yugui Yao A new experiment probes the quantum geometry of electronic wave functions involved in a nonlinear Hall response. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 246301] Published Mon Jun 15, 2026

item.title: Topologically nontrivial flat bands and quantum Hall crossovers in square-octagon lattice materials
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Amrita Mukherjee, Rahul Verma, Pritesh Srivastava, and Bahadur Singh
Author(s): Amrita Mukherjee, Rahul Verma, Pritesh Srivastava, and Bahadur Singh Coexistence of nontrivial topology and flat electronic bands provides a fertile platform for correlated quantum states. The square-octagon lattice hosts Dirac nodes and flat bands at half-filling, yet the effects of intrinsic spin-orbit coupling (SOC) and staggered magnetic flux on its electronic an… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 235420] Published Mon Jun 15, 2026
